what: Apple-Inspired Liquid Glass Design for Shiny Applications
title: Apple-Inspired Liquid Glass Design for Shiny Applications
text: Apple’s Liquid Glass aesthetic R Shiny — one function, built bslib.

title: Apple-Inspired Liquid Glass Design for Shiny Applications
Apple-Inspired Liquid Glass Design for Shiny Applications
text: Save app.R run shiny::runApp(): need shiny shinyglass. need load bslib — glass_theme() returns bslib theme object fluidPage() (Shiny page functions) understand automatically. Load bslib want UI helpers like card() page_fillable(). Standard Shiny inputs, buttons, layouts work box.
code: library(shiny) library(shinyglass)  ui <- fluidPage(   theme = glass_theme(),   titlePanel("Liquid Glass"),   selectInput("color", "Favorite color", c("Blue", "Purple", "Orange")),   sliderInput("n", "Number of bars", 5, 30, 15),   plotOutput("plot") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$plot <- renderPlot({     barplot(       seq_len(input$n),       col = "#007AFF",       border = NA,       main = paste("You chose", input$color)     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Apple-Inspired Liquid Glass Design for Shiny Applications
code: glass_theme(   preset     = "dark",   # "light" or "dark"   primary    = "#007AFF",   blur       = 28,   saturation = 200 )

what: Apple Liquid Glass theme for Shiny — glass_theme
title: Apple Liquid Glass theme for Shiny — glass_theme
text: Returns [bslib::bs_theme()] object styled Apple's Liquid Glass aesthetic. Pass `fluidPage()`, `navbarPage()`, bslib-aware page function; need.

title: Apple Liquid Glass theme for Shiny — glass_theme
code: glass_theme(   preset = c("light", "dark"),   primary = "#007AFF",   blur = 28,   saturation = 200,   radius = "1.25rem",   ... )

title: Apple Liquid Glass theme for Shiny — glass_theme
text: preset `"light"` `"dark"`. Controls overall color scheme. primary Primary accent color. Defaults Apple system blue (`#007AFF`). blur Backdrop blur radius pixels. saturation Backdrop saturation percentage. radius Default border radius glass surfaces. ... Additional arguments forwarded [bslib::bs_theme()].

title: shinyglass 0.1.0
text: Initial release. glass_theme() returns ‘bslib’ theme Apple-inspired Liquid Glass styling ‘shiny’ applications.
